raw.objective: “to develop a general method for calculating normalized drug concentrations in the presence of altered plasma protein binding”
raw.observations: The newly developed equation allows correcting for temperature and albumin concentration in calculating the free fraction of phenytoin. It was validated by comparison to the clinically validated Winter-Tozer equation. Its main advantage is that it should work for concentrations measured at temperatures other than the typical 37 degrees celsius
raw.equations: K1=theta1 K2=theta2 A0=-Cto/(K1*K2) A1=(1+Cmo*(Bc1*K1+Bc2*K2)-Cto*(K1+K2))/(K1*K2) A2=(Bc1+Bc2)*Cmo-Cto+1/K1+1/K2 Q=(3*A1-A2**2)/9 R=(9*A2*A1-27*A0-2*(A2**3))/54 if((Q**3+R**2)>=0) Cf=((R+sqrt(Q**3+R**2))**(1/3))+ ((R-sqrt(Q**3+R**2))**(1/3))-A2/3 else Cf=2*sqrt(-Q)*cos(arccos(R/sqrt(-Q**3))/3)-A2/3 Ctn=Cf+(Cmn/Cmo)*(Cto-Cf) Cmn is the normal total level of protein (mol/L) Cmo is the observed total level of protein (mol/L) Cto is the observed total concentration of drug (mol/L) Bc1 and Bc2 are number of binding sites for class 1 and 2 respectively, for PHT here those should be 1 and 1.

extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “to develop a general method for calculating normalized drug concentrations in the presence of altered plasma protein binding”

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
